free_text: Elastic and inelastic scattering of 270 MeV 3He
particles from 58Ni,90Zr,116Sn and 208Pb
Academic Sinica,Beijing
Indiana University Cyclotron Facility
The targets have thicknesses ranging from 10 to 100
mg/cm2
The quadrupole-dipole-dipole-multiple(QDDM)
magnetic spectrometer. The differential cross-section
measurements were carried out in the angular range of
theta ~ 6 degrees to 32 degrees in steps of 0.75
degrees or 1.5 degrees. The angular resolution was
kept at 0.2 degrees for theta < 27 degrees and 0.4
degrees for theta > 27 degrees.
Compiled as D6132 by S.Badwar,B.M.Jyrwa,
North Eastern Hill University,Shillong,Meghalaya,India.
On. Converted from EXFOR D6132.
The elastic scattering cross section have
absolute errors of the order of +- 5% arising mainly
due to i)target non uniformity and ii) uncertainties
in target thickness.
Plotted in Figure 1 of J,PRM,27,(6),747,1986

208Pb or for 116Sn.
The data have uncertainties  between
5 and 10% composed of errors due to i)counting
statistics  ii)target thickness and target non
uniformity and  iii)peak fitting procedure followed in
the data  analysis of overlapping levels.
